title: NOC 21109 – Other Professional Occupations in Physical Sciences | Canada Immigration with GTR Immigration
description: Professionals in physical sciences—including materials scientists, metallurgists, soil scientists, and aerospace specialists—support advanced manufacturing, environmental research, and government scientific programs across Canada. Classified under NOC 21109, this TEER 1 professional occupation continues to experience strong national demand—creating attractive immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Research metal and alloy properties and production methods
Study soil composition, distribution, and evolution
Investigate ceramics, composites, and advanced materials
Conduct space missions and scientific experiments
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in metallurgy, physics, chemistry, or related sciences
Master’s or doctoral degree for many research roles
Advanced professional licenses or applied-science degrees for some occupations
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 10,100 new positions for professionals in physical sciences, while around 9,600 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and advanced-degree immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Other Professional Occupations in Physical Sciences remain in demand across advanced manufacturing, government research agencies, universities, and industrial R&D centres nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is expected to continue.
Federal and provincial government departmentsUniversities and research institutesManufacturing and industrial firmsMaterials and metallurgy laboratoriesEnvironmental and agricultural research centres
Advanced-manufacturing developmentClean-technology researchInfrastructure materials innovationEnvironmental sustainability programsAerospace and space-science initiatives
Annual salaries typically range between $52,700 and $126,600 CAD, depending on province, discipline, and seniority.
